text: The surgeon re-emulsifies the polydocanol foam for twenty seconds before injecting two cc of foam into each hemorrhoidal pile.

The surgeon performs the procedure using a modified Blanc Blanchard technique, injecting polydocanol foam tangentially into the hemorrhoidal pile with a self-lighting open-ended anoscope and a XXG needle. The injection is made above the dentate line to minimize postoperative complications. The same technique is applied to the other two piles.
